title: SQL: Drop column (which is a primary key with foreign keys)
poc: create table t1(id int, name varchar(1024));
alter table t1 add constraint id_p primary key(id);
create table t2(id_f int, age int, foreign key(id_f) references t1(id));
alter table t2 add constraint id_p primary key(id_f);
key_bug_features: Dropping primary key column with foreign key references
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(id INT, name VARCHAR(1024), age INT DEFAULT NULL);
ALTER TABLE t1 ADD CONSTRAINT id_p PRIMARY KEY(id);
CREATE TABLE t2(id_f INT, salary DECIMAL(10, 2), FOREIGN KEY(id_f) REFERENCES t1(id));
ALTER TABLE t2 ADD CONSTRAINT id_f_p PRIMARY KEY(id_f);
ALTER TABLE t1 DROP COLUMN id;
